audits.conclusion: Gate A/B reproduced, but the original queue-deletion behavior and four opt-in regressions required repair/reclassification.
Naturally matured prediction suppresses explained sensory activity, but does not repair L2 ownership or collision dynamics.
The original independent gate enumeration was real, but decoder indexing, clearing, carryover, and exactly-once coverage had gaps.
Queue carryover repair, provenance telemetry, Gate A/B, default-off equivalence, and emergent maturity ordering were independently verified.
A local residual-driven selective L2 switch can fit existing step ordering, with accumulation safeguards and explicit negative controls.
PC-to-local-I suppression causally affects later sensory activity but cannot alter the already-resolved same-step L2 competition.
Scheduled events now survive switches exactly once; direct production maturity follows the established saturating quadratic update.
A one-step first-spike path can precede rivals in 19/29 tied seeds; 10/29 already need same-step symmetry breaking.
Introduced explicit basal/apical dendrites, local coincidence, sparse decoder learning, and paired PC output with default-off compatibility.
The paired/defer-once path was an external Phase 29 monkeypatch, not a native repaired-lineage mechanism, so no comparison was run.
All genuine secondary race spikes occurred before shared L2I threshold crossing; delivery delay and residual charge were not primary.
Decoder-source fragmentation is driven primarily by multiple L2 responders receiving credit before shared inhibition resolves.
At 25,600 steps, 11 clean complete pattern decoders emerge, but per-seed pattern coverage remains incomplete.
The original oracle found zero counterexamples over its bounded two-event domain, before later review corrected contract gaps.
Longer exposure improves decoder coverage, but persistent L2 ownership collisions prevent robust complete four-pattern coverage.
Production matched physical gating/locality but exposed real switch-queue loss and an oracle learning-equation mismatch.
Maturity eventually occurs, delayed by elapsed time and 4–6-source fragmentation; one seed reinforces a collided owner.
Reduced the true queue-loss defect and demonstrated that maturity divergence came from the oracle's linear update, not production.
At 3,200 steps no decoder matures; local updates and repaired stale carryover are correct but source-specific exposure is diffuse.
The corrected oracle agrees with repaired production on gating, learning, maturity ordering, carryover, locality, and default-off behavior.
